companyNameJapanese: 上海積塔半導体有限公司
description: Shanghai-based specialty foundry focusing on automotive, industrial, and power semiconductor chips. State-owned (华大半导体/CEC subsidiary). Operates two fab sites: Lingang (临港) and Xuhui (徐汇), Shanghai. Total capacity 300K wafers/month (8-inch equivalent): 6-inch 70K, 8-inch 110K, 12-inch 50K, SiC 30K wafers/month. Certified IATF16949 for automotive. Specializes in MCU, analog, power devices, sensors. Unicorn valuation: 31B CNY (Hurun 2025, #212 globally).

majorSuppliers: ACCRETECH/Tokyo Seimitsu (CMP-0001) - probing, dicing equipment for multi-size fabs
Domestic and imported semiconductor equipment suppliers
_externalRelationships.notes: Valuation 31B CNY (~651B JPY). Private, not yet listed. Raised 13.5B CNY in mega round (2023) - one of China's largest semiconductor fundraises that year. Major investors include national funds, industry investors, and local government funds. SiC capacity of 30K wafers/month is significant - China's largest SiC foundry capacity. 12-inch fab at Lingang for advanced power devices. As a multi-size-wafer foundry (6/8/12 inch + SiC), requires diverse probing and dicing equipment. ACCRETECH serves Chinese foundries of all sizes. Focus on automotive chips positions GTA as critical in China's push for domestic automotive semiconductor self-sufficiency.

_metadata.dataSource: Company website, Baike Baidu, 36Kr, Hurun, QQ News, Chinese language sources
